title: Create Event Ledger foundation
description: Introduce unified Event Ledger types/schemas and server storage at .fabric/events.jsonl. Add constants, append/read helpers, stable event envelope, typed event discriminators for existing ledger/audit concepts, and correlation/session id fields where appropriate. Do not migrate callers yet except tests for the new service.
test: Unit tests for event schema parse, append/read JSONL, malformed-line tolerance, and .fabric/events.jsonl path constant.
acceptance_criteria: New Event Ledger service writes and reads .fabric/events.jsonl; existing legacy files are untouched; typed events cover rule_context_planned, rule_selection, rule_sections_fetched, edit_intent_checked, rule_drift_detected, rule_baseline_accepted, mcp_event; tests pass.
hints: Use existing ledger-entry/audit-log schema style and read-ledger JSONL tolerance patterns || packages/shared/src/schemas/ledger-entry.ts;packages/server/src/services/read-ledger.ts;packages/server/src/services/audit-log.ts;packages/server/src/services/_shared.ts
execution_directives: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared test -- --runInBand || true; pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test -- event-ledger --runInBand
findings: Added shared Event Ledger schemas and server .fabric/events.jsonl append/read service. Legacy ledger/audit files remain untouched. Focused Vitest, shared/server typechecks, and builds passed.
acceptance_met: New Event Ledger service writes/reads .fabric/events.jsonl, tolerates malformed JSONL, exposes path constant, includes required typed discriminators with envelope/correlation/session fields, and leaves legacy files untouched.

title: Project legacy ledger and audit views from Event Ledger
description: Refactor read-ledger and audit-log services so public callers can read compatible LedgerEntry and AuditLogEntry views from the unified Event Ledger while preserving legacy fallback reads during migration. Update /api/ledger, doctor audit window matching, history replay, rehydrate state, and SSE event watching to include .fabric/events.jsonl.
test: Tests for readLedger projection, readAuditLog projection/window matching, legacy fallback, /api/ledger compatibility, and /events watching events.jsonl.
acceptance_criteria: Existing dashboard/history/doctor consumers still receive compatible data; audit compliance works from event records; legacy .intent-ledger.jsonl and audit.jsonl can still be read as fallback; SSE reacts to .fabric/events.jsonl.
hints: Build on T1 event reader/writer; preserve existing API payloads in this task || packages/server/src/services/read-ledger.ts;packages/server/src/services/audit-log.ts;packages/server/src/api/events.ts;packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts
execution_directives: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test -- read-ledger audit-log doctor index --runInBand
findings: Projected legacy ledger/audit views from .fabric/events.jsonl, preserved legacy fallbacks/deduping, dual-wrote audit events, moved MCP replay storage to mcp_event with fallback, and added SSE watching for events.jsonl.
acceptance_met: readLedger/readAuditLog now merge Event Ledger projections with legacy files; /api/ledger, doctor audit, history/rehydrate via readLedger, MCP replay, and SSE ledger:appended compatibility are covered. Focused Vitest suite passed.

title: Instrument MCP interfaces with typed events
description: Add event recording to Fabric server MCP/service boundaries: plan-context records read-only planning events, rule-sections records selection/fetch events, get-rules legacy path if still present records rule access, and mutation/fix style services use typed events. Avoid using fab_append_intent as the recording path.
test: Service/tool tests assert typed Event Ledger records for plan_context, rule_sections, and existing audit-compatible rule selection flows.
acceptance_criteria: MCP calls write typed events with path, stable ids, diagnostics, reasons, and correlation/session ids where available; no task summary event is required; old audit-log writes are routed through Event Ledger compatibility.
hints: Do not remove deprecated tool registration here; focus on instrumentation for surviving interfaces || packages/server/src/tools/plan-context.ts;packages/server/src/services/rule-sections.ts;packages/server/src/services/audit-log.test.ts
findings: Implemented typed Event Ledger instrumentation for planContext, ruleSections, legacy getRules, and audit/appendIntent compatibility paths. Added optional correlation/session propagation and diagnostics payloads.
acceptance_met: MCP/service calls write typed Event Ledger events with target paths, stable ids, reasons, diagnostics, and optional correlation/session ids; audit-compatible legacy writes route through Event Ledger compatibility; deprecated tool registrations retained temporarily.

title: Implement doctor and sync-meta baseline event flow
description: Make rule text the source of truth in the implementation path: enhance doctor drift details, keep normal report read-only, and add explicit baseline acceptance/sync event recording from doctor fix or sync-meta when agents.meta.json hash baseline is updated.
test: Tests prove doctor report does not write events, explicit fix/sync writes rule_drift_detected/rule_baseline_accepted/baseline_synced events, and doctor error clears only after baseline sync or rollback.
acceptance_criteria: Doctor exposes enough drift detail for file/stable_id/expected_hash/actual_hash; sync-meta or doctor fix updates baseline and records typed events; normal doctor report remains non-mutating.
hints: Existing doctor --fix only migrates legacy ledger; keep read path clean || packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/sync-meta.ts;packages/shared/src/schemas/event-ledger.ts
execution_directives: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test -- doctor --runInBand; pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- sync-meta --runInBand
findings: Implemented typed baseline drift/sync flow. Compatible targeted commands passed, and server/CLI builds passed.
acceptance_met: Doctor report exposes file/stable_id/expected_hash/actual_hash and does not write events; doctor --fix and sync-meta record rule_drift_detected, rule_baseline_accepted, and baseline_synced; drift clears after baseline sync.

title: Deprecate fab_append_intent and fab_update_registry surfaces
description: Explicitly remove or mark deprecated the fab_append_intent and fab_update_registry MCP tools from user-facing registration and guidance. Update protected tokens, bootstrap templates, docs, tests, and command expectations to point to Event Ledger automatic typed events plus doctor/sync-meta baseline flow.
test: Tests for protected token lint, server tool registration, bootstrap snapshots/templates, and docs references no longer require fab_append_intent or fab_update_registry as core protocol tools.
acceptance_criteria: Newly generated guidance does not tell agents to call fab_append_intent or fab_update_registry; server registration tests reflect deprecation; docs describe .fabric/events.jsonl and automatic MCP typed events.
hints: Requirement says these two are explicitly deprecated. Coordinate with T3 by doing this after instrumentation decisions are in place || packages/shared/src/i18n/protected-tokens.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/lint-protected-tokens.test.ts;packages/server/src/index.test.ts

findings: Deprecated fab_append_intent/fab_update_registry in user-facing guidance while retaining compatibility registrations with deprecated descriptions. Bootstrap/protected-token policy now points to fab_plan_context, fab_get_rule_sections, .fabric/events.jsonl, and sync-meta/doctor baseline flow.
acceptance_met: New generated guidance no longer instructs agents to call fab_append_intent or fab_update_registry; server registration test asserts they are deprecated compatibility surfaces; docs describe .fabric/events.jsonl automatic typed events and sync-meta/doctor baseline acceptance.
